data.title: White House reins in OpenAI's GPT-5.6
data.요약정보: PLUS: Give your AI agent a credit card (safely) Read Online|Sign Up|Advertise Good morning, {{ first_name | AI enthusiasts }}.Anthropic isn’t the only one facing blowback for building a powerful AI model. OpenAI is now pretty much in the same position — for the same reason. The White House has asked the company to limit the release of its next major model, GPT-5.6, as it brings “Mythos-like” capabilities and requires thorough testing before wider availability to the public. data.title: An Interview with Figma CEO Dylan Field About Design and AI
data.요약정보: Learn MoreMember Forum Learn MoreMember Forum This week’s Stratechery interview is with Figma co-founder and CEODylan Field. Field was a Thiel Fellow who dropped out of Brown in 2012 to start Figma. Figma was born of a technical breakthrough that leveraged WebGL to deliver powerful graphical capabilities in the browser; the browser made Figma collaborative, what I callthe operating system of design. data.title: Quoting Dean W. Ball
data.요약정보: Simon Willison highlighted Antoine Buteau's curated compilation 'Lessons from Dean W. Ball,' a 60-plus quote profile ofDean W. Ball, Research Fellow at theMercatus Center(and incoming OpenAI Strategic Futures lead, starting July 6). The piece draws from Ball's newsletter Hyperdimensional and podcast appearances to document his governance stance: AI is better understood as a discovery than an invention; diffusion and public infrastructure matter more than model-level regulation; and state agility...

data.title: What happened after 2,000 people tried to hack my AI assistant
data.요약정보: 26th June 2026 - Link Blog What happened after 2,000 people tried to hack my AI assistant(via) Fernando Irarrázaval ran a challenge onhackmyclaw.comto see if anyone could leak secrets held by his OpenClaw test instance by sending it email. Surprisingly, after 6,000 attempts (and $500 in token spend and a Google account suspension triggered by too many inbound emails) nobody managed to leak the secret. The underlying model was Opus 4.6, with the following prompt: This matches something I've been ...
